instructions.instruction: Always add an explicit language tag to every fenced code block. Never leave triple backticks without a language identifier.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks to write or review Markdown containing code samples.
instructions.why_given: The skill explicitly prohibits fenced code blocks without language tags because untyped blocks reduce readability and tooling support.

instructions.instruction: Never jump heading levels. Heading levels must increase by exactly one step at a time (e.g., H2 → H3, not H2 → H4).
instructions.relevant_when: User is authoring or reviewing a Markdown document with nested sections.
instructions.why_given: The skill lists skipping heading levels as an anti-pattern that breaks document hierarchy and accessibility.

instructions.instruction: Keep list marker style consistent within a document. Do not mix `-`, `*`, and `+` markers in the same list or across lists in the same file.
instructions.relevant_when: User is creating or editing a document that contains bullet lists.
instructions.why_given: The skill prohibits mixing inconsistent list and table styles because style drift increases diff noise and hurts readability.

instructions.instruction: When a markdownlint violation occurs, fix the source content or apply a narrow, justified inline exception. Never disable broad rules globally to make lint pass.
instructions.relevant_when: User encounters a markdownlint failure and asks how to fix it.
instructions.why_given: The skill's deterministic workflow mandates fixing source content rather than suppressing rules, and the anti-patterns section explicitly prohibits global rule disabling.

instructions.instruction: Follow the deterministic workflow: (1) identify document type, (2) apply template and section order, (3) write content, (4) run markdownlint and fix all violations, (5) validate CI integration.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks to create a new Markdown document from scratch.
instructions.why_given: The skill defines this exact five-step workflow as the required process for producing lint-clean documentation.
